symbols.name: shortestPath
symbols.detail: [NEEDS_FIX] Path reconstruction omits `from` node when BFS terminates early: the `while (node !== undefined)` loop walks parent map but `from` has no parent entry, so the loop stops before prepending it.

findings.detail: Loop `while (node !== undefined)` terminates when `parent.get(node)` returns `undefined` — which happens at `from`, the BFS root that was never inserted into `parent`. The root node is never prepended, so the returned path starts at the second node, not `from`. Fix: change the termination condition so the root is included, e.g. walk until `node === undefined` with a post-increment step that still pushes `from`, or seed `parent.set(from, undefined)` and check `parent.has(node)` instead.

actions.description: Path reconstruction loop exits before prepending `from`. Either seed the parent map with `parent.set(from, undefined as any)` and guard with `parent.has(node)`, or change the termination from `node !== undefined` to `node !== from` and then push `from` after the loop. As-is, the returned array is missing its first element for all paths of length > 1.
